title: C. neoformans Electroporation Transformation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"mtgl","text":"Protocol for electroporation transformation of digested plasmid or PCR products into C. neoformans utilised in Hardwick lab. Adapted from \"Modified Transient CRISPR-Cas9 Coupled with Electroporation(TRACE)\", see references. Filter sterilised. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8gev4","text":"1M DTT","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1kaf","text":"Ice-cold ddH2O","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":12,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8j10n","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1rde5","text":"Materials ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"blq8g","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5bcvd","text":"Electroporation cuvettes: We use Cell Projects, 2mm Gap (Cat. EP-102)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7dgn6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: 2-3 days prior to transformation, recover strains of interest from -80C stocks, patching to YPDA plates.

1 day prior to transformation, prepare precultures from plated strains, and grow at 30 °C o/n to OD 600 0.2 on the morning of transformation. 100mL preculture provides enough biomass for 4-5 transformations.

Prepare selective medium plates in YPDA, typical concentrations of standard antibiotics shown in Table 1, below. Antibiotic Concentration 1 Geneticin (G418) 100µg/mL 2 Hygromycin B (HYG) 300µg/mL 3 Nourseothricin (NAT) 100µg/mL
On morning of transformation, check preculture OD and monitor growth at 30 °C to OD 600 0.3-0.36. Meanwhile pre-chill sterile ddH 2 O and EB-Buffer on ice.

Prepare DNA for transformation. For digested plasmid transformation 5-10µg DNA should be utilised, phenol-chloroform extraction ethanol precipitation recommended for cleanup, especially of large plasmids. Resuspended in 10µL ddH 2 O.
Centrifuge culture at 3200 rpm, 4 °C, 3 minutes to pellet cells, wash twice with 35mL ice-cold ddH 2 O . Cells can be kept on ice for a few hours if required.
All further steps should be carried out with ice-cold buffers and On ice .
Centrifuge washed cells at 3200 rpm, 4 °C, 3 minutes and resuspend in 10mL EB-Buffer , adding 1mM DTT. Incubate On ice 01:00:00 .
Centrifuge cells at 3200 rpm, 4 °C, 3 minutes , remove supernatant and resuspend in 250µL EB-Buffer . Should have roughly 500µL of cell slurry total.
To DNA On ice , add 40µL cell slurry and mix. Transfer to pre-cooled electroporation cuvette, being careful not to introduce bubbles.
Electroporate cells at 1.7kV, 600Ω, 25µF, pulse once and immediately place back On ice .
Add 1mL YPDA to electroporated cells, and transfer to 1.5mL eppendorf tube. Incubate 200 rpm, 30 °C, 2 hours , allowing cells to recover and begin expressing antibiotic resistance genes.

Spin down cells 2000 rpm, Room temperature, 3 minutes , remove majority of supernatant and resuspend in ~100µL remaining YPDA. Plate to antibiotic resistance plates, spreading with beads, and incubate 30 °C .
Check after 2 days (may take up to 3 days for colonies to appear), once colonies appear, pick and streak for single colonies onto antibiotic containing plates to ensure pure single colony strains.
Once colonies appear upon streaked plates, patch to YPDA and grow for downstream screening via PCR or western blot.
creator: Thomas Davies
